examples.text: You can hack only if you solved all versions of this problem. There are nnn communicating vessels of infinite height arranged in a circle. The base area of each vessel is 111 cm22^2, and between the iii-th vessel and the (imodn)+1(imodn)+1(i \bmod n) + 1-th vessel there is a connection of negligible volume at height hihih_i cm. For each vessel iii, find the maximum total volume of water in cm33^3 that can be placed in these vessels under the condition that the iii-th vessel remains empty. Formally, you are given an array h1,h2,…,hnh1,h2,…,hnh_1, h_2, \ldots, h_n.
